title: Guided Playground Action Stack
concept: A beginner-card, single-column progressive disclosure redesign for documentation writers preparing demos. The active unsaved Playground appears first, followed by grouped action cards that reveal exact launch, save, management, Site Manager, Blueprint gallery, and portability consequences inline.
focus: single-column progressive disclosure instead of a split operations console
beginner action cards with concrete inputs, progress, and result states
monochrome interface with colored status chips for temporary, saved, destructive, and informational states
documentation writer workflow for preparing, saving, inspecting, and exporting demo sites
flows: Choose distinct launch routes for Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, and .zip import with route-specific inputs and consequences.
Save an unsaved Playground to browser storage or a local directory with separate destination cards, copy progress, and resulting saved identity.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with visible temporary state, rename, delete, and delete consequence messaging.
Adjust runtime settings with older versions, PHP, language, network, multisite, destructive reset, and stored Save & Reload behavior.
Use Site Manager tools for settings, file browser, Blueprint editing and bundle actions, database tools, logs, Homepage, WP Admin, export to GitHub, and download as .zip.
Browse the Blueprint gallery with category filters, search, selected detail, inspect, copy URL, and run behavior while representing the 43-entry catalog.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
